task_text: Prepare a comprehensive project health briefing for NovaBridge's leadership team. The briefing should cover: current progress, key risks, upcoming Q3 commitments, and any issues that need leadership attention. Save it as project_health_briefing.txt.

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS

Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===

TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Project Helios — Q2 Budget vs. Actuals
Prepared by: NovaBridge Finance | Date: 2026-07-07

Category,Q2 Budget ($),Q2 Actual ($),Variance ($),Notes
Engineering (internal),180000,194200,+14200,Overrun: unplanned sprint added in May
Subcontractors,45000,51800,+6800,GeoSync integration scoping work
Cloud infrastructure,28000,31500,+3500,Load testing environment spun up early
QA and testing,22000,20100,-1900,Under budget; QA resource joined 2 weeks late
Travel and client meetings,12000,9400,-2600,Two site visits deferred to Q3
Contingency reserve,15000,15000,0,Untouched
TOTAL,302000,322000,+20000,

Projected additional Q3 overruns:
- GeoSync license (if not renewed before expiry): $12,000
- UAT extension (1 additional week): $15,000
- Estimated total H2 projected overrun: $47,000

Note: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
CFO review scheduled: 2026-07-18
Action required: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

Project Helios — Team Roster

Name: Tomas Rivera      | Title: Frontend Engineer      | Email: t.rivera@novabridge.com

input.content:
NovaBridge Leadership Briefing — Project Helios Health
Prepared: 2026-07-11
Audience: NovaBridge Leadership Team

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Project Helios is currently assessed as ON TRACK with material execution risks requiring active management. Core technical progress is strong in several areas: the API gateway is complete, authentication testing is passing, and the data ingestion module is substantially built. However, the project is entering a high-risk phase ahead of the confirmed Meridian product demo on 2026-07-25 and Q3 UAT commitments. The most significant concerns are Meridian source data quality, unresolved GeoSync licensing, incomplete integration/UAT readiness, dashboard performance, and budget pressure.

Leadership attention is recommended on four items: 1) ensure the GeoSync license is resolved before the demo, 2) reinforce focus on high-severity ingestion defects and performance benchmarking, 3) support cross-company escalation if Meridian environment access or sample data slips, and 4) review the emerging Q3/H2 budget overrun and reconciliation plan.

CURRENT PROJECT HEALTH
Overall status: ON TRACK, with minor-to-moderate risks trending upward
Current phase: Late build / integration preparation
Next major external milestone: Meridian product demo — 2026-07-25
Target launch date: 2026-10-01

Progress by workstream:
- API gateway: 100% complete and delivered.
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete; blocked/at risk due to Meridian date-format inconsistencies and high-volume ingestion failure.
- Client dashboard: 60% complete; dashboard test coverage is still early, with several charting and benchmark cases not yet run.
- Integration testing: 40% complete; integration test sign-off remains targeted for 2026-07-25.
- Authentication QA: 4/4 test cases passed.
- Data pipeline QA: 5/7 batch ingestion tests passed; 2 high-severity failures remain open.
- Dashboard QA: 1/4 test cases complete; load-time benchmark has not yet run.

Q2 deliverables status:
Completed:
- Requirements documentation v1.0
- System architecture design
- API gateway implementation
- Development environment setup
- Data schema finalization

In progress:
- Data ingestion module — target 2026-07-18
- Client dashboard v1 — target 2026-07-22

Overdue / rescheduled:
- Integration test suite setup — originally due 2026-06-30, rescheduled to 2026-07-20
- UAT environment provisioning — originally due 2026-07-01, blocked by Meridian IT access
- Performance benchmarking report — originally due 2026-07-05, owner: Derek Okafor

KEY RISKS AND ISSUES
1. Meridian source data quality and date-format inconsistency
- Status: Active blocker / confirmed in Sprint 14
- Severity: High
- Impact: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY inputs, affecting approximately 40% of Meridian source data.
- Current dependency: Meridian to provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Risk: Delay could affect live-data demo readiness and integration sign-off.
- Owner: Amir Hassan; Meridian dependency with Priya Kapoor/Meridian IT.

2. Large-file ingestion failure
- Status: Active defect
- Severity: High
- Impact: Pipeline crashes around the 210k-record mark; Q3 performance target is 500k records/min sustained.
- Risk: Could compromise demo credibility and later performance-hardening commitments.
- Owner: Amir Hassan; root cause under investigation.

3. GeoSync mapping library license renewal
- Status: Active risk
- Target resolution: 2026-07-14
- Impact: Demo may be jeopardized if the license is not renewed before 2026-07-25.
- Current ownership discrepancy: Risk register lists Raj Patel; meeting action assigns Derek Okafor. Ownership should be clarified immediately.
- Leadership need: Ensure Legal/commercial review is completed by the 2026-07-14 target.

4. Dashboard performance at scale
- Status: Active risk
- Impact: Dashboard load-time benchmark for 100k records has not yet run; target is under 2 seconds.
- Related overdue item: Performance benchmarking report was due 2026-07-05.
- Owner: Derek Okafor
- Risk: Performance concerns were raised by Meridian stakeholders and must be addressed before the demo.

5. UAT timeline slippage
- Status: Active risk
- Current plan: UAT period extended by one week to 2026-08-17 through 2026-08-28.
- Impact: A Q3 UAT delay of more than two weeks could push launch into Q4 and trigger Section 7.1 penalty review.
- Risk level: High likelihood, medium impact.
- Owner: Sarah Chen

6. Meridian IT environment access delays
- Status: Active risk
- Current dependency: Meridian IT infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12; UAT environment access expected by 2026-07-19.
- Impact: Blocks UAT provisioning and could delay integration environment setup.
- Owner: Nadia Petrov; Meridian dependency with Priya Kapoor.

7. Budget pressure
- Q2 budget: $302,000
- Q2 actuals: $322,000
- Q2 variance: +$20,000 over budget
- Main Q2 overrun drivers: internal engineering +$14,200, subcontractors +$6,800, cloud infrastructure +$3,500.
- Projected additional Q3 overruns: GeoSync license exposure of $12,000 and UAT extension of $15,000.
- Estimated H2 projected overrun: $47,000.
- Constraint: Per MSA Section 4.2, internal cost overruns cannot be passed to Meridian Group.
- CFO review: 2026-07-18
- Budget reconciliation plan due: 2026-07-20

8. Key person dependency
- Status: Monitored
- Issue: Derek Okafor is a key technical dependency, with no backup technical lead identified.
- Impact: Derek owns or is linked to several high-priority items, including performance benchmarking and GeoSync license follow-up from meeting actions.
- Leadership consideration: Identify backup technical leadership coverage for Q3 critical path.

UPCOMING Q3 COMMITMENTS
July — Complete system integration
- Finalize data ingestion module by 2026-07-18.
- Complete client dashboard v1 by 2026-07-22.
- Complete integration test sign-off by 2026-07-25.
- Complete integration test suite setup by 2026-07-20.
- Resolve GeoSync license by 2026-07-14.
- Obtain Meridian clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15.
- Obtain Meridian IT infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12.
- Confirm UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

July 25 — Product demo for Meridian
Confirmed demo expectations:
- Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data.
- End-to-end pipeline walkthrough.
- Dashboard visualization with real data, per contract Section 9.3.
- Meridian sponsor James Whitfield specifically emphasized that the demo must use live data, not mock data.

August — User Acceptance Testing
- UAT window: 2026-08-17 to 2026-08-28.
- Support Meridian user testing sessions.
- Address UAT feedback, with two sprints budgeted.
- Current risk: UAT has already been extended by one week; additional slippage could threaten launch timing.

August-September — Performance hardening
- Dashboard load-time target: under 2 seconds for 100k records.
- Ingestion throughput target: 500k records/min sustained.
- External security penetration test: vendor still TBD.

September — Launch preparation
- Production environment setup.
- Runbook documentation.
- Hypercare plan finalization for a 30-day post-launch model.
- Final client training sessions.

Target launch
- Planned launch date: 2026-10-01.
- Key launch risk: UAT slippage of more than two weeks may push launch into Q4 and trigger penalty review under contract Section 7.1.

LEADERSHIP ATTENTION REQUIRED
1. Resolve GeoSync licensing by 2026-07-14
- Ask: Confirm ownership between Legal, Derek Okafor, and Raj Patel; remove ambiguity and ensure renewal is completed before the demo.
- Decision/Action: Leadership sponsor may be needed if Legal review stalls.

2. Protect the 2026-07-25 Meridian demo
- Ask: Treat the demo as a critical external milestone with daily readiness tracking through 2026-07-25.
- Watch items: live Meridian sample data, date normalization fix, large-file ingestion stability, dashboard visualization readiness, and performance benchmark results.

3. Escalate Meridian dependencies if dates slip
- Ask: Be prepared to escalate to Meridian sponsor James Whitfield if the clean sample dataset, infrastructure diagram, or UAT access is late.
- Dependency dates: infrastructure diagram due 2026-07-12, clean sample dataset due 2026-07-15, UAT access due 2026-07-19.

4. Review Q3 budget exposure and reconciliation plan
- Ask: Finance and project leadership should align ahead of CFO review on 2026-07-18.
- Key issue: Q2 is already $20,000 over budget, with projected H2 overrun of $47,000. Internal overruns are not recoverable from Meridian.
- Deliverable: Budget reconciliation plan due to CFO by 2026-07-20.

5. Address technical leadership concentration risk
- Ask: Assign a deputy technical lead or explicit backup for Derek Okafor during the July critical path.
- Rationale: Several high-impact items are dependent on Derek or require his oversight, including benchmarking, demo readiness, and license follow-through.

6. Confirm performance-hardening ownership and plan
- Ask: Require a near-term plan for dashboard benchmark completion and large-file ingestion remediation.
- Rationale: Performance concerns have already been raised by Meridian and are central to Q3 success metrics.

RECOMMENDED NEXT STEPS
Within 48 hours:
- Confirm GeoSync license owner and escalation path.
- Validate whether Meridian delivered the IT infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12.
- Establish daily demo-readiness standup through 2026-07-25.
- Assign backup technical lead coverage for critical July activities.

By 2026-07-15:
- Confirm receipt of Meridian clean sample dataset.
- Re-test date normalization for MM/DD/YYYY inputs.
- Provide leadership a defect status update on HEL-D-014 and HEL-D-015.

By 2026-07-18:
- Finalize data ingestion module or provide variance explanation.
- Send draft demo agenda to Meridian.
- Prepare for CFO review with a budget reconciliation narrative.

By 2026-07-20:
- Complete integration test suite setup.
- Submit budget reconciliation plan to CFO.
- Confirm UAT environment provisioning status.

By 2026-07-22:
- Complete client dashboard v1.
- Complete dashboard benchmark testing or document remediation plan.

By 2026-07-25:
- Complete integration test sign-off.
- Conduct Meridian product demo with live ingestion and real-data dashboard visualization.

BOTTOM LINE
Project Helios remains viable for the current Q3 plan and 2026-10-01 launch target, but the margin for slippage is narrowing. The most urgent threats are not broad delivery failure, but concentrated blockers around data quality, licensing, environment readiness, performance validation, and budget governance. Focused leadership intervention over the next two weeks can materially reduce risk before the Meridian demo and August UAT window.

content.text: Done — I prepared the comprehensive project health briefing and saved it as `project_health_briefing.txt`.
